Notice Of Motion And Motion For Admission Of Alexander S. Elson To The Bar Of This Court Pro Hac Vice
Off Calendar Oct-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. MELISSA MONTENEGRO, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP (Tel: 650-697-6000), JOHN W. BLACK, Esq. of Black & Buffone PLLC (Tel: 202-670-0403), and ALEXANDER S. ELSON, Esq. and EMILY GOLDMAN, Esq. of National Student Legal Defense Network (Tel: 202-734-7495);
Atty. PATRICK HAMMON, Esq. of McManis Faulkner (Tel: 408-279-8700) for defendant Bloom Institute of Technology and Austen Allred.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EMILY BRUNER's Motion For Admission Of Alexander S. Elson To The Bar Of This Court Pro Hac Vice.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

Noting the absence of this information in plaintiff's motion papers, the Court inquires as to how many cases in California Mr. Elson has applied for pro hac vice admission in within the last two years. Mr. Elson responds that he has only applied for pro hac vice admission in one other case.

The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Noting the absence of this information in plaintiff's motion papers, the Court inquires as to how many cases in California Mr. Black has applied for pro hac vice admission in within the last two years. Mr. Black responds that he has only applied for pro hac vice admission in this current case.

The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
